title: ranunculus hyperboreus subsp. samojedorum
intro: Ranunculus hyperboreus subsp. samojedorum, also known as the northern ranunculus, is a rare, alpine plant species native to the mountainous regions of northern Europe, particularly in Russia, Scandinavia, and the Faroe Islands. The plant contains ranunculin, a toxic compound with medicinal properties, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. The health benefits of Ranunculus hyperboreus subsp. samojedorum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are typically used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds, while its roots can be used to prepare decoctions for internal use.
